heading: Student aid index for independent students without dependents other than a spouse
label: 1087pp Student aid index for independent students without dependents other than a spouse
notes: Amendments2020— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to family contribution for independent students without dependents other than a spouse.Pub. L. 116–2602007—Subsec. (b)(1)(A)(iv). amended cl. (iv) generally. Prior to amendment, cl. (iv) read as follows: “an income protection allowance of the following amount (or a successor amount prescribed by the Secretary under )—Pub. L. 110–84section 1087rr of this title“(I) $6,050 for single students;“(II) $6,050 for married students where both are enrolled pursuant to subsection (a)(2) of this section; and“(III) $9,700 for married students where one is enrolled pursuant to subsection (a)(2) of this section;”.2006—Subsec. (b)(1)(A)(iv). , substituted “$6,050” for “$5,000” in subcls. (I) and (II) and “$9,700” for “$8,000” in subcl. (III).Pub. L. 109–171, § 8017(b)(1)(A)Subsec. (c)(4). , substituted “20” for “35”.Pub. (b)(1)(A)(iv). , in introductory provisions, substituted “allowance of the following amount (or a successor amount prescribed by the Secretary under )—” for “allowance of—”.Pub. (b)(1)(A)(iv)(III). , substituted “$8,000” for “$6,000”.Pub. L. 105–244, § 474(b)(3)1997—Subsec. (b)(1)(A)(vi). added cl. (vi).Pub. L. 105–781993—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. (A) which read as follows: “computing the student’s available taxable income by deducting from the student’s adjusted gross income—”. Former subpar. (B) redesignated (C).Pub. (C) and inserted, after subpar. (C), provision that the student’s income supplemental amount from assets not be less than zero.Pub. L. 100–50, § 14(17)Subsec. (c)(2)(B). , substituted “displaced homemaker” for “dislocated homemaker”.Pub. L. 100–50, § 14(3)Subsec. (c)(2)(C). , added table and struck out former table which read as follows:Pub. children.heading: Computation of student aid index
children.label: a Computation of student aid index
A
children.text: the family’s available income (determined in accordance with subsection (b)); and

children.label: B
children.text: the family’s available assets (determined in accordance with subsection (c)).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: If the sum determined under paragraph (1) with respect to an independent student without dependents other than a spouse is less than − $1,500, the student aid index for the independent student shall be − $1,500.

children.label: ii
children.text: an allowance for payroll taxes, determined in accordance with paragraph (2);

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a single independent student without dependents, $14,630, as adjusted pursuant to ; andsection 1078rr(b) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a married independent student without dependents, $23,460, as adjusted pursuant to ; andsection 1078rr(b) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of a married independent student, an employment expense allowance, as determined in accordance with paragraph (3); and

children.label: B
children.text: multiplying the amount determined under subparagraph (A) by 50 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: the total amount earned by the student (and spouse, if appropriate), multiplied by the rate of tax under ; andsection 3101(b) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: the amount earned by the student (and spouse, if appropriate) that does not exceed such contribution and benefit base (twice such contribution and benefit base, in the case of a joint return) for the year of the earnings, multiplied by the rate of tax applicable to such earnings under .section 3101(a) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: If the student is married, such allowance is equal to the lesser of $4,000 or 35 percent of the couple’s combined earned income (as adjusted by the Secretary pursuant to ).section 1087rr(g) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: If the student is not married, the employment expense allowance is zero.

children.label: i
children.text: the difference between the family’s assets (as defined in ) and the asset protection allowance (determined in accordance with paragraph (2)); multiplied bysection 1087vv(f) of this title

children.heading: Not less than zero
children.label: B Not less than zero
children.text: The family’s available assets under this subsection shall not be less than zero.

children.heading: Computations in case of separation, divorce, or death
children.label: d Computations in case of separation, divorce, or death
children.text: In the case of a student who is divorced or separated, or whose spouse has died, the spouse’s income and assets shall not be considered in determining the family’s available income or assets.
